content.problemStatement:
You are given a family tree with NNN members numbered 1...N1 ... N1...N. Every person iii, except for the founder of the family (root) has a parent denoted by P[i]P[i]P[i]. P[root]P[root]P[root] = -1 by definition. Person iii is a descendant of person jjj if iii belongs to the subtree rooted at jjj.

The net worth (adjusted for inflation) is given for all the members of the family. Your task is to find the maximum difference in net worth's of two members where one member is a descendant of the other.

You can assume that no two members of this family married each other. So it remains a "family tree".


Input Format

First line will contain  an integer NNN, denoting the number of members.
Second line contains N space seperated integers W[1], W[2] ... W[N] denoting the net_worth of the N members.
Third line contains  N space seperated integers P[1], P[2] ... P[N] denoting the parents of the N members.


Output Format
Output in a single line the maximum difference in net worth's of any two members where one member is a descendant of the other.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer N, denoting the number of members in the family tree.
The second line contains N space-separated integers W[1], W[2], ..., W[N] denoting the net worth of the N members.
The third line contains N space-separated integers P[1], P[2], ..., P[N] denoting the parents of the N members.
content.outputFormat: The maximum difference in net worth's of any two members where one member is a descendant of the other.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The maximum difference occurs between the 3rd and 4th members. W[3] - W[4] = 6
The maximum difference occurs between the 1st and 5th members. W[5] - W[1] = 40
The maximum difference occurs between the 1st and 3rd members. W[3] - W[1] = 20

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we will use a depth-first search (DFS) approach to traverse the family tree. We will maintain two variables for each node: the maximum net worth of its descendants and the minimum net worth of its descendants. We will update these variables as we traverse the tree. The maximum difference in net worth's of any two members where one member is a descendant of the other will be the maximum difference between the net worth of a node and the minimum net worth of its descendants.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) — where N is the number of members in the family tree. This is because we visit each node once during the DFS traversal.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — where N is the number of members in the family tree. This is because in the worst case, the tree can be skewed to one side, resulting in a recursion call stack of size N.

code.Python3:
import sys
max_diff = 0

def dfs(node, net_worth, parent, min_descendant, max_descendant):
    global max_diff
    if parent[node] == -1:
        min_descendant[node] = net_worth[node]
        max_descendant[node] = net_worth[node]
    else:
        dfs(parent[node], net_worth, parent, min_descendant, max_descendant)
        min_descendant[node] = min(net_worth[node], min_descendant[parent[node]])
        max_descendant[node] = max(net_worth[node], max_descendant[parent[node]])
    max_diff = max(max_diff, max_descendant[node] - min_descendant[node])

N = int(input())
net_worth = list(map(int, input().split()))
parent = list(map(lambda x: int(x) - 1, input().split()))
min_descendant = [0] * N
max_descendant = [0] * N
for i in range(N):
    dfs(i, net_worth, parent, min_descendant, max_descendant)
print(max_diff)
